trivia_bullets.text: Common Minotaur Warrior, THB original print only. Skophos is the minotaur polis of Theros — located in the far northern canyons of Phoberos, most devoted to Mogis, God of Slaughter; Skophos's center is the largest Mogis temple on the plane.
sources: MTG Wiki: Skophos + Mogis via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: "The minotaur polis of Skophos boasts grand temples to the god of slaughter." Direct lore-anchor: the flavor text names the setting depicted, making this figure a warleader of that specific polis, not a generic minotaur.

The sacrifice ability ({R}, sacrifice a creature or enchantment: +1/+0 and menace) mechanically mirrors Mogis's doctrine of slaughter-as-tribute — Skophos minotaurs offer lives to their god and grow stronger for it. Art by Svetlin Velinov.
sources: MTG Wiki: Mogis via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Hipsters of the Coast common review grade D: 'mediocre ability that can randomly win the game in very specific scenarios.' The 4/5 body for 5 mana is solid but the sacrifice activation requires board resources and additional mana. Legal in Pauper.
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: THB Common Review 2020-01

related_cards.name: Mogis, God of Slaughter
related_cards.note: Patron deity of Skophos whose temples appear in this card's background

related_cards.name: Portent of Betrayal
related_cards.note: Companion Mogis-flavored card; steal + sacrifice combo piece

related_cards.name: Infuriate
